message:
beads: close ft-xbnl0 (all 5 child epics + 2 blockers closed, goal-line rollup complete)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572605Z","close_reason":"Landed in bf854b26 (fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0). Short-circuit DedupeVerdict::New at top of check() when max_capacity == 0, mirroring ft-bx4le's pattern for SignalDeduplicator. Regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 pins the behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6269x","title":"[reality-gap] ft snapshot restore Unix-only status is not disclosed in README session-persistence matrix","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:128 Session Persistence matrix row:

    | Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite |
    Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and `ft session doctor`
    are implemented, but live restore still depends on the current
    WezTerm mux interop boundary. \`ft restart\` is currently
    Unix-only. |

Disclosed: `ft restart` Unix-only.
Undisclosed: `ft snapshot restore` has the *same* cfg gate.

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27100` (ft restart — disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:39715` (ft snapshot restore — NOT disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft snapshot restore is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

Same gate, same failure mode. README line 128 specifically mentions
`ft restart` as the Unix-only surface but omits `ft snapshot restore`,
which is also mentioned in the *same* matrix row ("Snapshots, session
inspection, **restore**, and `ft session doctor` are implemented").

A Windows operator reading the matrix is told "restore is
implemented" (true, on Unix) without the cross-platform caveat.

# Failure shape

Operator on Windows runs `ft snapshot restore ...` → eprintln hits
→ exit 1. Workaround unknown.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:128 to say:

    Snapshots and session inspection are implemented cross-platform;
    `ft restart` and `ft snapshot restore` are currently Unix-only.

One-line doc change. Alternative: fix the cfg gate. The session
persistence code under `#[cfg(unix)]` likely depends on real Unix
signals / fork semantics — porting is non-trivial. Docs-first is
the pragmatic path.

# Related

ft-2guux previously flagged G2 "`ft restart` Unix-only not in
support matrix" and was closed 2026-04-21 with a README.md:128
update. That update added disclosure for `ft restart` but missed
`ft snapshot restore` — this bead is the follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane8","owner":"pane3-readme-fix","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:55.113196Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.619147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208098Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","platform","reality-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-682n6","title":"Migrate ~50 tokio-based async tests to asupersync runtime (ft-22x4r tracked)","description":"# Goal
Convert ~50 tests using #[tokio::test] to work with the asupersync runtime. These tests fail at runtime with: task::spawn called outside of Runtime::block_on context. Originally tracked as ft-22x4r.

# Background
The asupersync migration replaced tokio, but ~50 tests still use #[tokio::test]. Count verified 2026-04-06: 50 in .rs files (excluding .bak). Most are in *_labruntime.rs test files (2 per file across ~25 files) plus 2 in dependency_eradication.rs.

# Design
## Discovery (exact count at implementation time)
1. grep -r '#\[tokio::test\]' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v '.bak' > /tmp/tokio_tests.txt
2. Count and categorize by file pattern:
   - *_labruntime.rs files: ~25 files × 2 tests = ~50 tests (async smoke + basic scenario)
   - dependency_eradication.rs: 2 tests
3. For each, identify tokio primitives used: spawn, sleep, channels, timeout

## Migration strategy
- *_labruntime.rs pattern: These all follow the same template (2 tests per file). Once one is migrated, the rest are mechanical.
  Replace: #[tokio::test] with #[test] + runtime_compat::block_on(async { ... })
  Or: if asupersync provides a test macro, use that
- Replace tokio::spawn -> runtime_compat spawn equivalent
- Replace tokio::time::sleep -> runtime_compat::sleep
- Replace tokio channels -> runtime_compat channels

## Batch approach
Since *_labruntime.rs files follow a uniform pattern, write a migration helper that does the mechanical replacement, then verify each file compiles.

## Structured Logging
- Migration log: file, old_macro, new_macro, primitives_replaced, compile_status

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Zero #[tokio::test] in .rs files (verified by grep, excluding .bak).
2. All ~50 previously-failing tests now pass.
3. No semantic changes — same assertions.
4. Total test count preserved.
5. No new tokio dependencies.
6. Commit message lists affected files.

# Implementation Notes
- runtime_compat.rs provides bridge primitives.
- The *_labruntime.rs naming convention suggests these are lab/smoke tests for async behavior.
- This bead unblocks ft-xbnl0.2 (async cutover epic).
- Coordinate with ft-n2j5w (MCP test fix) if any MCP tests also use tokio::test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:21.227738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.155157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.154811Z","close_reason":"Verified: zero actual #[tokio::test] annotations remain in crates/frankenterm-core/. All 50 occurrences found by grep are in comments/docstrings/string literals describing the migration that was already done. The *_labruntime.rs files already use #[test] + RuntimeFixture::current_thread().block_on(). No code changes needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","migration","testing"]}
-{"id":"ft-6gqga","title":"[stub] workflows/wait_execution.rs:199-204: WaitCondition::External permanently returns Unsupported","description":"# Genuine production stub

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/wait_execution.rs:199-204` — `WaitConditionExecutor::execute_wait` arm for `WaitCondition::External { key }`.

```rust
WaitCondition::External { key } => {
    // External signals are not implemented in this layer
    Ok(WaitConditionResult::Unsupported {
        reason: format!("External signal '{key}' requires external signal registry"),
    })
}
```

This is not a platform stub (the pattern-wait and timeout-wait arms above it are fully implemented) — `External` is a first-class variant of the public `WaitCondition` enum but the execution path has NEVER been wired. Every caller that constructs `WaitCondition::External { key }` silently gets `Unsupported` back. The error hint names 'external signal registry' as a missing dependency, but no such registry exists anywhere in the crate (`rg 'external.*signal.*registry' crates/frankenterm-core/src` returns zero hits).

# Scope — NOT the same as the frankensqlite family

Recorder/ingest/reindex FrankenSqlite stubs are tracked under the BLOCKED wa-2l27x.8 FrankenSQLite migration plan and were narratively disclosed via ft-trby3. The `WaitCondition::External` stub has no parent bead — it's an orphaned production gap.

# Fix options

1. **Remove the variant.** If no caller actually constructs `External`, the enum should not expose the variant. Git blame shows the variant was added speculatively; `rg 'WaitCondition::External' crates` finds only the match arm and the enum declaration — no producer. Cleanest fix: delete the variant + match arm, file the follow-up when an external-signal feature is actually designed.

2. **Implement the registry.** Introduce a `SignalRegistry` trait with pub fn `signal(key: &str)` / `wait(key: &str, timeout: Duration) -> future`. Thread it through `WaitConditionExecutor::new`. Non-trivial — requires designing signal lifetime/TTL semantics.

Recommend option (1) pending a real consumer. Either way, `WaitConditionResult::Unsupported` has no legitimate emit site after the fix.

# Acceptance

Either `WaitCondition::External` is removed from the public enum + all tests updated, OR the executor actually waits on a registered external signal and the integration is covered by a test that registers, fires, and observes the signal.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:45:23.924074Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:45:23.924074Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-6gqga","title":"[stub] workflows/wait_execution.rs:199-204: WaitCondition::External permanently returns Unsupported","description":"# Genuine production stub

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/wait_execution.rs:199-204` — `WaitConditionExecutor::execute_wait` arm for `WaitCondition::External { key }`.

```rust
WaitCondition::External { key } => {
    // External signals are not implemented in this layer
    Ok(WaitConditionResult::Unsupported {
        reason: format!("External signal '{key}' requires external signal registry"),
    })
}
```

This is not a platform stub (the pattern-wait and timeout-wait arms above it are fully implemented) — `External` is a first-class variant of the public `WaitCondition` enum but the execution path has NEVER been wired. Every caller that constructs `WaitCondition::External { key }` silently gets `Unsupported` back. The error hint names 'external signal registry' as a missing dependency, but no such registry exists anywhere in the crate (`rg 'external.*signal.*registry' crates/frankenterm-core/src` returns zero hits).

# Scope — NOT the same as the frankensqlite family

Recorder/ingest/reindex FrankenSqlite stubs are tracked under the BLOCKED wa-2l27x.8 FrankenSQLite migration plan and were narratively disclosed via ft-trby3. The `WaitCondition::External` stub has no parent bead — it's an orphaned production gap.

# Fix options

1. **Remove the variant.** If no caller actually constructs `External`, the enum should not expose the variant. Git blame shows the variant was added speculatively; `rg 'WaitCondition::External' crates` finds only the match arm and the enum declaration — no producer. Cleanest fix: delete the variant + match arm, file the follow-up when an external-signal feature is actually designed.

2. **Implement the registry.** Introduce a `SignalRegistry` trait with pub fn `signal(key: &str)` / `wait(key: &str, timeout: Duration) -> future`. Thread it through `WaitConditionExecutor::new`. Non-trivial — requires designing signal lifetime/TTL semantics.

Recommend option (1) pending a real consumer. Either way, `WaitConditionResult::Unsupported` has no legitimate emit site after the fix.

# Acceptance

Either `WaitCondition::External` is removed from the public enum + all tests updated, OR the executor actually waits on a registered external signal and the integration is covered by a test that registers, fires, and observes the signal.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:45:23.924074Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:48:04.604803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6m0gs","title":"mux pane-output drain handles callback reentry","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:09.863968Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:58:13.344669Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:58:13.344339Z","close_reason":"Verified by pane_output_reentrant_enqueue_is_drained_before_returning","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6mxfs","title":"[mor:security-engineering] ws_dir = root.join('.ft','patterns') is not canonicalized before discovery","description":"# Observation

In `load_packs_from_config`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:949):

    let ws_dir = root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns");
    let discovered = discover_packs_from_dir(&ws_dir)?;

ft-fbee5 (just closed) fixed the case where a symlink INSIDE
`.ft/patterns/` escapes the sandbox. But the parent path is itself
just a join, no canonicalize. A hostile cloned repo can ship:

    .ft/patterns -> /etc

(a symlink at the directory level.) `ws_dir` canonicalizes to
`/etc`, `discover_packs_from_dir(/etc)` iterates there, and the
per-file sandbox check passes because it canonicalizes with root =
`/etc`. The symlink escape is now one level up.

Similar attack surface for `config.resolved_user_packs_dir()` if
that ever returns a joined path the caller doesn't canonicalize.

# Question

Should `load_packs_from_config` canonicalize `ws_dir` and verify the
result resolves *inside* `root_path.canonicalize()` before calling
`discover_packs_from_dir`? That closes the parent-level symlink
vector without needing to change `discover_packs_from_dir`'s
contract.

Stretch: is there a case for centralizing this as a
`sandbox_resolve_dir(dir, root)` helper and using it everywhere the
pack/workflow/policy loaders join a workspace-relative path? The
pattern is probably not unique to pack loading.

# Trust boundary

The repo is the attacker; the user's home directory is the victim.
Everything a hostile repo can make `read_to_string` see is a
potential data-exfil primitive. Treat repo-relative path
construction as crossing a trust boundary, and canonicalize at the
boundary crossing, not at the leaf.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:09.320199Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.174562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:59.305207Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0239fbd6 — added sandbox_resolve_dir helper, routed load_packs_from_config's ws_dir through it with 4 regression tests (missing, inside-root, toplevel-symlink-escape, end-to-end).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","sandboxing","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -275,7 +275,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-x4k2b","title":"[reality-gap] runtime_compat 'audited boundary' scope understated — 709 call sites across 83 files","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:38-42:

    The runtime model is asupersync-native in the product-facing
    APIs: structured, cancel-correct, and centered on Cx-aware
    orchestration across search, policy, workflows, robot mode,
    and the feature-gated web/distributed surfaces. The remaining
    watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses a named
    runtime_compat quarantine while the last task-spawn seams are
    being collapsed.

…and:

    `runtime_compat` remains as an audited boundary for runtime
    lifecycle, channel, time, and blocking semantics; it is not
    the product story.

The framing is: small, named, collapsing.

# Reality (code)

    rg -c "runtime_compat::" crates/frankenterm-core/src/
      → 709 call sites across 83 files

The boundary isn't a narrow interop shim — it's the default way
product code spawns tasks, opens channels, sleeps, and times out.
Every capture loop, every workflow step, every web handler, every
mux-pool operation transits it. The "named quarantine" framing
implies 1-2 modules; the code implies every subsystem.

# Why this matters

- **Trust calibration**: operators and contributors use the README
  to calibrate what's "native asupersync" vs. "transitioning." A
  readme that says "the boundary is small" while 83 files sit on
  it gives the wrong picture of where the project is on its own
  milestone (ft-xbnl0).
- **Cancellation semantics**: asupersync's Cx-first path is
  behaviourally different from runtime_compat's tokio fallback
  (see ft-xbnl0 epic for the formal distinction). Callers reading
  the README might assume their task spawns are Cx-aware when
  they're not.
- **Migration progress tracking**: ft-xbnl0.2.x has been landing
  fixes at the task-spawn level for weeks. The README should track
  which specific paths have crossed the threshold and which are
  still on runtime_compat, so operators can calibrate expectations.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:38-42 with either a count ("runtime_compat is
still load-bearing for ~700 call sites across 83 files; the
ft-xbnl0 epic tracks collapse"), or a list of the specific
product surfaces still on runtime_compat (watch daemon,
capture loops, workflow steps, web handlers).

Either form gives operators a calibrated picture without
overstating or understating the state.

# Related

- ft-2guux G1 raised the same concern about the watch daemon
  specifically; closed with a README clarification.
- ft-xbnl0 is the open epic tracking the full migration. This bead
  is specifically about the README framing, not the migration itself.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:45:20.507726Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:56.093751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:50:22.624765Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap","runtime"]}
 {"id":"ft-x50ce","title":"[MEDIUM] conformal::ConformalPredictor silently narrows prediction intervals when coverage=NaN","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/conformal.rs:286` — `ConformalPredictor::new` stores `coverage: coverage.clamp(0.01, 0.999)`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _) = NaN` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5. When `coverage = NaN`, the stored value stays NaN. On every `forecast_all` call:

1. `cal.quantile(self.coverage)` (line 326) is called with NaN coverage.
2. `CalibrationBuffer::quantile` (line 213) computes `k = ((coverage * (n+1)).ceil() as usize).max(1)` → `NaN.ceil() = NaN` → `NaN as usize = 0` → `k = 1`.
3. `k > n` is false (n ≥ 1), so falls through.
4. Returns `sorted[0]` — the **smallest** calibration score.
5. `forecast_all` wraps `(point - q, point + q)` using the smallest q possible — producing the **narrowest** prediction interval the calibration data can support.

**Net**: operators set `coverage = NaN` (via config typo, arithmetic bug upstream, deserialization of an invalid input), and the predictor silently undercuts the formal-coverage guarantee by emitting the tightest possible intervals instead of the widest. Intervals labelled `coverage: NaN` in the output look broken but are still shipped; downstream alerting thresholds driven by these intervals over-fire.

## Severity rationale

- Silent statistical wrong-answer: the predictor's entire point is the formal coverage guarantee; NaN undermines it silently.
- Not a crash or data loss.
- Input reachable via config deserialization or external arithmetic (e.g., `1.0 - alpha` where alpha came from a NaN upstream).

## Fix

Guard at the new() boundary: if `coverage.is_nan()`, snap to the default 0.95 (same as `ConformalConfig::default()` at line 76). Alternative would be to reject and return Result, but callers signature is fn new() -> Self, so keep the infallible default. A tracing::warn log helps debug.

Also tighten `CalibrationBuffer::quantile` at line 213 so even if `self.coverage` is ever NaN (future code paths, direct callers), the function returns None instead of silently-smallest. Same pattern as ft-b4l62/ft-jz40p.

## Tests

* `ft_x50ce_predictor_rejects_nan_coverage_at_construction` — `new(..., coverage: NaN, ..)` stores 0.95 default, not NaN.
* `ft_x50ce_quantile_nan_coverage_returns_none` — `CalibrationBuffer::quantile(NaN)` returns None (defense in depth).

## Scope

Single file (`conformal.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:29:12.968247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:02.964336Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:02.963917Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-x5tx2","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:25:58.707216Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.588832Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.587715Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0","title":"[EPIC] Goal-line closure for FrankenTerm: native Asupersync, zero fake capabilities, and multi-day swarm reliability","description":"# Goal
Close the original FrankenTerm mission all the way to a credible finish line: a swarm-native terminal and control plane that can replace fragile legacy multi-agent terminal workflows, survive large multi-day agent workloads, and stop depending on dishonest capability claims or legacy async runtime debt.

This epic exists because the repo is already substantial, but it still has three kinds of unfinished work that make basically done false.
1. Native async migration is incomplete; direct `tokio`, `smol`, `async-io`, and `runtime_compat` quarantine still shape real behavior.
2. Several supported-path surfaces still advertise capabilities that are stubbed, placeholder-quality, or explicitly `todo!()` / `unimplemented!()`.
3. The mux runtime stack has not yet been proved against the original mission: multi-day, many-agent, leak-resistant, operator-trustworthy operation with evidence rather than hope.

# Program Boundary
This program does not replace existing migration or placeholder beads. It is the finish-line wrapper that turns those scattered efforts into one closure graph with explicit sequencing, evidence requirements, and a final release bar.","design":"# Sequencing
1. Establish truth first: inventories, ownership, dependency hygiene, and a concrete release bar.
2. Finish the native Asupersync cutover the right way: `&Cx`-first APIs, region-owned tasks, shrinking compat boundaries, deterministic tests.
3. Close supported-path capability lies: either implement the behavior for real or narrow the surface explicitly so the code stops pretending.
4. Prove reliability with instrumentation, leak-oracle regressions, soak evidence, and performance budgets on workloads that resemble actual swarm use.
5. Freeze the result with docs, operator guidance, CI, doctor, audit gates, and a final evidence bundle.

# Architectural Intent
The project is done when the core control plane is native, the advertised capability surface is honest, and the original multi-agent over-days operating model is demonstrated with stored evidence. +{"id":"ft-xbnl0","title":"[EPIC] Goal-line closure for FrankenTerm: native Asupersync, zero fake capabilities, and multi-day swarm reliability","description":"# Goal
Close the original FrankenTerm mission all the way to a credible finish line: a swarm-native terminal and control plane that can replace fragile legacy multi-agent terminal workflows, survive large multi-day agent workloads, and stop depending on dishonest capability claims or legacy async runtime debt.

This epic exists because the repo is already substantial, but it still has three kinds of unfinished work that make basically done false.
1. Native async migration is incomplete; direct `tokio`, `smol`, `async-io`, and `runtime_compat` quarantine still shape real behavior.
2. Several supported-path surfaces still advertise capabilities that are stubbed, placeholder-quality, or explicitly `todo!()` / `unimplemented!()`.
3. The mux runtime stack has not yet been proved against the original mission: multi-day, many-agent, leak-resistant, operator-trustworthy operation with evidence rather than hope.

# Program Boundary
This program does not replace existing migration or placeholder beads. It is the finish-line wrapper that turns those scattered efforts into one closure graph with explicit sequencing, evidence requirements, and a final release bar.","design":"# Sequencing
1. Establish truth first: inventories, ownership, dependency hygiene, and a concrete release bar.
2. Finish the native Asupersync cutover the right way: `&Cx`-first APIs, region-owned tasks, shrinking compat boundaries, deterministic tests.
3. Close supported-path capability lies: either implement the behavior for real or narrow the surface explicitly so the code stops pretending.
4. Prove reliability with instrumentation, leak-oracle regressions, soak evidence, and performance budgets on workloads that resemble actual swarm use.
5. Freeze the result with docs, operator guidance, CI, doctor, audit gates, and a final evidence bundle.

# Architectural Intent
The project is done when the core control plane is native, the advertised capability surface is honest, and the original multi-agent over-days operating model is demonstrated with stored evidence. Compile success alone is not a finish line.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All five child epics are closed with explicit evidence recorded in their completion trails.
2. Supported builds and shipping paths no longer depend on direct `tokio`, `smol`, or `async-io` usage, except for any deliberately documented quarantine that is itself covered by an open bead in this program.
3. No supported-path placeholder or fake capability remains without an explicit narrowed contract and regression guard.
4. Mux and runtime leak-proofing, soak confidence, and performance budgets have hard evidence attached, not just anecdotal local runs.
5. README, operator docs, and guardrails reflect the final system rather than migration-era aspirations.
6. Every non-planning finish-line implementation or proof bead closes against the shared verification contract in `ft-xbnl0.1.4`, with exact `rch exec -- ...` proof commands and stored artifacts.","notes":"Use this epic as the authoritative wrapper for the final push. Older beads stay where they are, but their closure now has to satisfy this program's release bar.

All non-planning children of this program should treat `ft-xbnl0.1.4` as the shared verification contract. Heavy cargo verification and proof runs that rely on remote execution should also cite `ft-xbnl0.1.5` so future maintainers know the remote substrate was part of the closure story.

Canonical contract document: `docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md`.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:07:15.833371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:48:02.595502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:48:02.595334Z","close_reason":"All 5 child epics and blockers closed - goal-line program rollup complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","mux","release-readiness","reliability","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:59.919450Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:59.698012Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2816,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","author":"IndigoBear","text":"Sequencing rule for the whole finish-line program:
1. Establish truth inventories and normalize the backlog.
2. Finish native Asupersync cutover.
3. Close supported-path fake/unimplemented behavior.
4. Prove the original multi-day swarm mission with leak instrumentation, deterministic regressions, and soak evidence.
5. Freeze the result with docs, guards, operator acceptance, and a final closure bundle.

The project is not “done” at compile success. It is done only when all five layers are complete.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:53Z"},{"id":2817,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Created from the goal-line planning request to replace dependence on the old markdown plan with a bead-native finish-line program.

1. The new graph intentionally wraps inherited blockers instead of replacing them, so existing implementation work remains traceable.
2. Two important gaps that were not cleanly represented before now have explicit ownership: the remaining mux-server `sessionhandler` operations and the long-haul reliability proof lane.
3. The root finish-line claim is stricter than compile success: native Asupersync closure, honest support claims, leak remediation, soak proof, release guards, and operator acceptance all have to land.","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:26:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.1","title":"[EPIC] Goal-line control: truth inventory, backlog normalization, and closure ledger","description":"# Goal
Establish a trustworthy execution map for the finish-line push so work is not guided by stale beads, grep folklore, or half-remembered architecture notes.

This epic exists because the remaining work is spread across old async-migration beads, the placeholder-remediation program, and uncovered gaps like mux-server sessionhandler behavior and leak-proofing. Before implementation, future agents need one self-contained ledger that answers four questions.
1. What is still unfinished?
2. Which surfaces are genuinely supported?
3. Which older beads are still authoritative?
4. What evidence is required before any lane can be called done?","design":"# Design
1. Build two truth inventories: one for async and runtime debt, one for supported-path placeholder and unimplemented debt.
2. Normalize the older graph rather than pretending it does not exist; link or de-duplicate beads instead of creating shadow plans.
3. Add an explicit evidence matrix so later closure is testable and auditable.
4. Keep this epic operational: it should be updated as new finish-line facts are discovered.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every finish-line blocker is mapped either to an inherited bead or a concrete new bead in this program.
2. Every inherited dependency used by this program has current status, scope, and rationale that still make sense.
3. The finish-line evidence bar is written down in bead-native form, not only in chat history or markdown plans.
4. New work picked from this program can be understood without reopening the old markdown plan.","notes":"This is the control epic, not a paperwork tax. Its output should actively reduce wasted implementation effort and prevent done-in-one-place, still-open-somewhere-else confusion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:51.657113Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T01:59:27.278872Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T01:59:27.278640Z","close_reason":"All 5 child beads closed (1.1-1.5): async/runtime inventory complete, supported-path audit complete, closure ledger normalized, verification contract defined, RCH worker parity stabilized.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","inventory","ledger","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:51.657113Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","title":"Inventory every direct tokio, smol, async-io, and compat surface and assign a native Asupersync owner","description":"# Goal
Produce the authoritative async-debt ledger for the finish-line push.

This task exists because the project still has multiple overlapping sources of truth for runtime debt: `Cargo.toml` manifests, direct imports, `runtime_compat`, hidden helper modules, and comments that describe old migration assumptions. The finish-line graph needs one bead-native inventory that names each active offender, where it lives, why it still exists, and which downstream bead owns removing it.","design":"# Design
1. Search manifests and source for direct `tokio`, `smol`, `async-io`, and compat-layer usage.
2. Classify each occurrence as one of three things: native replacement required, temporary quarantine boundary, or dead code or comment debt that should simply disappear.
3. Record the intended Asupersync replacement shape for each class, including whether the migration should use `&Cx`, `Scope`, native networking, native channels, or explicit adapter quarantine.
4. Link every inventoried surface to an inherited or newly-created owner bead.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every direct async-runtime offender relevant to supported builds is mapped to a file or crate location, a migration class, and an owner bead.
2. The inventory distinguishes real shipping-path debt from test-only or intentionally quarantined holdouts.
3. The inventory is attached to this bead in durable bead-native form and cross-linked from downstream cutover tasks.
4. No downstream async bead in this program relies on undocumented mystery debt.","notes":"SilentSpire 2026-04-03: authoritative async-debt ledger for the finish-line async cutover.

Shipping-path classes
A. Direct vendored/client Smol + async-io surfaces -> native replacement required -> owner `ft-xbnl0.2.1` (inherits `ft-riz42`).
   files: `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`; `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`; `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`; `frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs`; `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs`; `frankenterm/ssh/src/lib.rs`; `frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs`; `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
   active usage shape confirmed by source search: `smol::Async`, `smol::channel`, `smol::future::or`, `smol::block_on`, `smol::Timer`, `smol::io::*`, plus `async_io::IoSafe` wrappers and `async_io::Timer` / `block_on` executor seams.
   replacement shape: native Asupersync socket or FD registration, `&Cx`-threaded APIs, scope-owned tasks, native channels, native timers, and no executor-neutral Smol shims.

B. Core Tokio quarantine in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs` -> temporary quarantine boundary to collapse -> primary owner `ft-xbnl0.2.2`, with transport/network sub-owners `ft-xbnl0.2.3` and `ft-xbnl0.2.4`, and final seam removal `ft-xbnl0.2.5`.
   root compat surface still covers locks, channels, spawn/join, time, Unix/TCP sockets, signal handling, and runtime builder utilities.
   current import blast radius: 77 files under `crates/frankenterm-core/src` + `crates/frankenterm/src` import `runtime_compat`; that count includes hot-path code plus audit/guard helpers, so ownership is partitioned below.
   `ft-xbnl0.2.2` hot-path files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs`, `events.rs`, `search_bridge.rs`, `watchdog.rs`, `telemetry.rs`, `restore_process.rs`, `session_retention.rs`, `survival.rs`, `retry.rs`, `wait.rs`, `workflows/mod.rs`, `workflows/runner.rs`, `workflows/engine.rs`, `caut.rs`, `tui/query.rs`.
   `ft-xbnl0.2.3` local transport files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs`, `native_events.rs`, `event_stream.rs` and related local control-stream helpers.
   `ft-xbnl0.2.4` network boundary files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`, `web/server.rs`, `web/sse.rs`, `web_framework.rs`.
   replacement shape: thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs, replace detached spawn with `Scope`, replace Tokio-shaped channels/locks/select/sleep with native Asupersync primitives, and leave only explicit named quarantine where a foreign boundary is still unavoidable.

C. Manifest and feature-seam debt -> owner `ft-xbnl0.2.5` for cleanup/collapse and `ft-xbnl0.2.6` for permanent enforcement.
   manifests still carrying direct runtime debt in supported paths or verification scaffolding: root `Cargo.toml` (`tokio`, `async-io`, `smol`, dev `criterion` `async_tokio`), `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml` (`tokio.workspace = true`, dev `criterion` `async_tokio`), `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/Cargo.toml` (`async-io.workspace = true`, `smol.workspace = true`), `frankenterm/client/Cargo.toml` (`async-io.workspace = true`, `smol.workspace = true`, `async-io` and `async-smol` feature wiring), `frankenterm/promise/Cargo.toml` (`async-io.workspace = true`), `frankenterm/codec/Cargo.toml` (`smol` optional + `async-smol` feature), `frankenterm/ssh/Cargo.toml` (`smol` optional + `async-smol` feature), `frankenterm/uds/Cargo.toml` (`async-io` optional feature), `frankenterm/async_ossl/Cargo.toml` (`async-io` optional feature).
   propagated feature-seam cleanup that must collapse with the same lane: `frankenterm/config/Cargo.toml`, `frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml`, `frankenterm/lua-api-crates/termwiz-funcs/Cargo.toml`, and `frankenterm/lua-api-crates/mux-lua/Cargo.toml` still forward `async-smol` / Smol-linked seams.
   replacement shape: remove dependency declarations and dual-runtime feature seams only after lanes `2.1`-`2.4` land; then make zero-`tokio`/`smol`/`async-io` a permanent repo gate in `2.6`.

Non-shipping / guard / comment debt
- No live non-quarantine `tokio::` APIs were found outside `runtime_compat.rs` in supported shipping code.
- Remaining `tokio::` references outside the quarantine root live in `dependency_eradication.rs`, `forbidden_dep_guards.rs`, `manifest_dep_eradication.rs`, `runtime_compat_surface_guard.rs`, `vendored_async_contracts.rs`, and token-scan guard logic in `frankenterm/ssh/src/lib.rs`.
- Owner split: `ft-xbnl0.1.3` normalizes inherited meaning and closure state; `ft-xbnl0.2.6` turns these into durable actionable guards/doc sweeps instead of stale reminders.

Inherited blocker map
- `ft-riz42` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.1`
- `wa-1m7nk` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.2`
- `wa-16hou` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.3` (note: ProudSalmon already landed a bounded `ipc.rs` support slice on 2026-04-01; do not duplicate it)
- `wa-1u55z` + `wa-3mfv9` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.4`
- `wa-2h1ha` -> absorbed by `ft-xbnl0.2.6`

Bottom line
- Real shipping-path async debt is fully partitioned across `ft-xbnl0.2.1` / `.2.2` / `.2.3` / `.2.4` / `.2.5` / `.2.6`.
- `ft-xbnl0.1.3` owns inherited-ledger normalization; `ft-xbnl0.1.4` owns the shared proof bar each cutover lane must satisfy.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:06.307225Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-04T00:57:40.446072Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T00:56:15.187408Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cutover","goal-line","inventory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:06.307225Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2825,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","author":"SnowyCastle","text":"Validated the async-debt inventory against the current workspace on 2026-04-03.

Corroboration used:
- `mcp__morph_mcp__codebase_search` over remaining direct `tokio`, `smol`, `async-io`, and `runtime_compat` usage.
- `rg -n '(tokio|smol|async-io|runtime_compat)' Cargo.toml crates frankenterm docs README.md AGENTS.md`.

Confirmed owner partition remains accurate:
- direct vendored/client `smol` + `async-io` shipping-path debt -> `ft-xbnl0.2.1`
- core `runtime_compat` quarantine collapse -> `ft-xbnl0.2.2` / `.2.3` / `.2.4` / `.2.5`
- manifest and permanent guard cleanup -> `ft-xbnl0.2.5` / `.2.6`

Concrete corroborating surfaces still present in code today include:
- `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs` (`smol::Async`, `smol::channel`, `smol::future::or`, `async_io::IoSafe`)
- `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` and multiple `crates/frankenterm-core/src/*` modules importing `runtime_compat::*`
- workspace manifests still carrying direct runtime debt in the locations named in the bead notes

This leaves `ft-xbnl0.1.1` as a completed inventory/ownership ledger, not an implementation lane. Downstream finish-line async work should treat this bead's notes as the authoritative offender map and update them only if the offender set changes materially.
","created_at":"2026-04-04T00:56:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.1.2","title":"Audit every supported-path todo, unimplemented, and fake capability and classify its closure mode","description":"# Goal
Produce the authoritative supported-path truth inventory for finish-line work.

This task exists because not every `todo!()` matters equally. Some are test-only, some are unsupported-platform shims, and some are shipping-path lies that directly break operator trust. The project needs a durable inventory that says which gaps must be implemented, which must be narrowed honestly, and which are out of scope for good reasons.","design":"# Design
1. Audit supported-path surfaces across client control, robot mode, policy, FTUI, mux-server, tmux, SSH, rendering, and platform backends.
2. Classify each suspect as implement, narrow explicitly, declassify as unsupported by design, or exclude as test-only or vendored debt.
3. Cross-link each supported-path blocker to the placeholder-remediation program where possible and create new finish-line beads where no good owner exists.
4. Record user impact and safety impact so prioritization is grounded in actual operator risk.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every supported-path placeholder or unimplemented surface relevant to the finish line is mapped to an owner bead.
2. Unsupported-by-design cases have an explicit planned contract instead of remaining ambiguous.
3. Duplicate coverage between this program and `ft-akx00` is resolved by cross-link, not silence.
4. The inventory is sufficient for a future agent to understand what still lies about support and what does not.","notes":"SilentSpire 2026-04-03: authoritative supported-surface truth ledger for the finish-line capability program.

Finish-line scope boundary
- `ft-xbnl0.3` is the finish-line placeholder/capability epic. Its scope is machine-control/client truth, live scrollback and FTUI truth, mux-server sessionhandler operations, tmux/detach/SSH/remote-file semantics, renderer/window-backend truth, and the final supported-path honesty sweep.
- Broader `ft-akx00.3.*` operator-support cleanup and `ft-akx00.4.*` connector/search placeholder remediation remain real work, but they are not `ft-xbnl0` capability blockers unless the finish-line support matrix is explicitly widened.

Finish-line owner map
1. Control-plane/client truth -> `ft-xbnl0.3.1`
   supporting beads: `ft-akx00.2.3` (in progress: remote `spawn_pane` + authoritative alt-screen truth), `ft-akx00.5.4` (in progress: stale `PaneCapabilities` stub language).
   already closed supporting beads: `ft-akx00.2.1`, `ft-akx00.2.2`, `ft-akx00.2.4`.
   user-facing surfaces: client pane lifecycle, authoritative alt-screen, policy/machine-control capability truth.

2. Live scrollback / FTUI truth -> `ft-xbnl0.3.2`
   supporting beads: `ft-akx00.5.1` (in progress: real pane scrollback wiring), `ft-akx00.5.3` (open: live FTUI view bodies).
   already closed supporting bead: `ft-akx00.5.2` (recording delta placeholders removed by API collapse).
   declassification: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_scrollback_coordinator.rs` is now `#[cfg(test)]` only; the live blocker is finishing real scrollback/FTUI integration, not removing a production null object.

3. Mux-server pane/window operations still marked unimplemented -> `ft-xbnl0.3.3`
   this gap is not cleanly owned by `ft-akx00`; it is a finish-line-only bead for supported sessionhandler behavior in `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/sessionhandler.rs`.
   declassification: the noisy `FakePane` `unimplemented!()` methods in that file live under `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` and are not production blockers.

4. Tmux / detach / SSH / remote-file semantics -> `ft-xbnl0.3.4`
   supporting beads: `ft-akx00.7.2` (open: detach semantics), `ft-akx00.7.3` (open: SFTP metadata mutation), `ft-akx00.7.4` (open: SSH `Match Exec`).
   already closed supporting bead: `ft-akx00.7.1` (tmux PTY lifecycle).

5. Rendering / window-backend truth -> `ft-xbnl0.3.5`
   supporting beads: `ft-akx00.6.1` (open: Wayland seat/keyboard), `ft-akx00.6.2` (in progress: resize/framebuffer lifecycle), `ft-akx00.6.3` (open: bitmap/WebGPU readback), `ft-akx00.6.5` (open: background sizing + glyph placeholders).
   already closed supporting bead: `ft-akx00.6.4` (terminfo raw/cropped image rendering).

6. Final supported-path honesty sweep -> `ft-xbnl0.3.6`
   this is the last pass after `3.1`-`3.5` and should verify code, docs, and operator-visible error behavior all agree on what is actually supported.

Explicit exclusions / not finish-line blockers today
- `frankenterm/mux/src/pane.rs` and `frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs` `FakePane` `unimplemented!()` methods live under `#[cfg(test)]` modules.
- `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/sessionhandler.rs` `FakePane` `unimplemented!()` methods live under `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs::collect_system_memory()` returns `(0, 0)` only under `#[cfg(not(any(target_os = "linux", target_os = "macos")))]`; this is an unsupported-platform shim, not a supported-path finish-line blocker under the current support matrix.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs` still renders Python/TypeScript/Go client templates with `transport not wired`; the Rust SDK path is already closed by `ft-akx00.2.4`. Treat the non-Rust generated clients as broader SDK/template debt outside current `ft-xbnl0` scope unless the supported matrix explicitly adopts them.
- Connector/search placeholder work stays owned by `ft-akx00.4`: `ft-akx00.4.1` in progress, `ft-akx00.4.2` open, `ft-akx00.4.3` closed, `ft-akx00.4.4` closed.
- Operator-support placeholder work `ft-akx00.3.1` / `.3.2` / `.3.3` is already closed and does not block `ft-xbnl0.3`.

Bottom line
- Every finish-line-relevant supported-surface placeholder now maps cleanly to `ft-xbnl0.3.1` / `.3.2` / `.3.3` / `.3.4` / `.3.5` / `.3.6` plus named `ft-akx00.*` dependencies.
- Duplicate coverage between `ft-xbnl0` and `ft-akx00` is resolved by cross-link, not duplicate implementation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:06.660290Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:25:30.486733Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:25:30.486497Z","close_reason":"Attached supported-surface truth ledger and cross-linked finish-line capability owners","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capability-truth","goal-line","inventory","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:06.660290Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
